signals.description: Continuous similarity to short factual / lookup-shaped prompts. Math-flavored exemplars deliberately omitted — they directly hit proof prompts (q00666 had this detractor fire on a Lagrange-multipliers prompt), which is the opposite of intent.
candidates: Which artist sculpted David?
What is the largest country by area?
What does the acronym CPU stand for?
Define osmosis.
What is the chemical formula for water?
Who invented the telephone?
In what year did the Berlin Wall fall?
Translate 'hello' to Spanish.
Who is the current monarch of the United Kingdom?
What is the official language of Brazil?
Name the deepest ocean on Earth.
What does the acronym DNA stand for?
What is 7 plus 4?
What is 12 divided by 3?
What is half of 20?
What is 6 times 5?

signals.description: Continuous similarity to T2/T3-grade explanation, comparison, and basic-implementation prompts.
candidates: Explain how a database B-tree index handles range queries with a worked example.
Walk me through how a TLS handshake establishes a shared key — the cryptographic flow at a protocol level.
Explain Bayes' theorem using a concrete worked example like spam classification or medical screening.
Compare TCP, UDP, and SCTP — when to use each, and what trade-offs you're picking up.
What are the operational differences between microservices and modular monoliths for a mid-sized engineering team?
Compare reference counting and tracing garbage collectors — what each gets right and where they typically fail.
Implement a thread-safe LRU cache in Go with O(1) get and put operations.
Write a Python decorator that retries a function with exponential backoff on configured exception types.
Implement a Bloom filter in Python with configurable false-positive rate.
Walk me through how to diagnose an event-driven Node.js backend with a slow memory leak under steady-state load.
Explain what 'eventual consistency' actually means at the application level, with the operational implications that matter to developers.
Walk through how a confusion matrix is computed for a multi-class classifier, including precision, recall, and F1 per class.
Solve a probability problem involving combinatorics and conditional events over a finite sample space.
Run a hypothesis test on whether a sample proportion differs significantly from a stated baseline.
Compute a business break-even point given fixed costs, variable costs, and gross margin.
Analyze how a stated percentage shift in inputs propagates through a multi-component budget or scoring formula.

signals.description: Continuous similarity to T5-grade frontier prompts.
candidates: Take a contested position on a long-standing debate within a field, anticipate the strongest counter-argument, and refute it without retreating to consensus or hedging.
Construct an original evaluative framework for a domain where consensus methodologies are widely criticized. Take the strongest critique seriously and produce actionable recommendations.

Construct a defensible position that contemporary management consulting as an industry is net-negative for the organizations it serves. Anticipate and respond to the strongest objection without retreat.

Argue rigorously for the position that the term 'mental health' as used in workplace HR has become incoherent. Discriminate this claim from anti-therapy and anti-mental-health-awareness positions. Propose a constructive replacement framework.

Walk this person through whether to remain in academia or leave for industry: 38-year-old researcher with two children, ten years from tenure, meaningful but underfunded research agenda. Refuse the considerations-list response. Commit to a recommendation.

Review this Raft-adjacent leader-election implementation. Reconstruct the author's mental model, identify where it diverges from canonical Raft, walk through the worst-case operational scenario, and propose minimum fixes.

Read this exponential-backoff retry decorator. The author was clearly attempting a named algorithm from a well-known blog post. Identify what they intended, where the implementation diverges, and what operational symptom would distinguish this from a correct one.

Propose specific regulatory mechanisms for how a US federal agency should govern healthcare AI deployment over the next three years, with rule-text-level specificity. Identify the weakest part of your own proposal and explain why.

Critique this paper's claim that scaling laws hold across modality boundaries. Identify three structurally distinct methodological problems. For each, propose a modification AND identify the new problem the modification introduces.
Translate this Mandarin poem into idiomatic English while preserving the original's cadence and tonal patterns. Write a half-page paratext explaining your three hardest translation decisions and why.

Diagnose what's actually happening at a 400-person scaleup that has just lost three senior engineers in two months. Leadership disagrees on cause. Engage with this as organizational analysis with specific signals to trust, not as a strategy framework.

Construct an original ethical framework that takes seriously both the effective-altruist and the communitarian critiques rather than synthesizing them. Test your frame against the canonical thought experiments each tradition has used.

signals.description: Continuous similarity to judgment/commitment-shape T4 prompts (decide, recommend, diagnose, take-a-position). Threshold lowered to 0.58 so discursive 'discuss the considerations'-shape prompts also match (e.g., ethical/philosophical considerations queries) — they're argumentative-adjacent even when not commit-shaped.
candidates: Make the case for a specific architectural pattern over its alternatives. Don't survey — commit to one.
Help me think through whether to fire an underperforming team member. Engage with this specific situation.
Pick one of these design options and tell me why. No surveys, no hedging.
Should we adopt this framework across the org? Take a position and defend it.
Recommend a specific book that would help someone in this situation. Just one.
Make a call: yes or no, and explain your reasoning. No 'it depends'.
Diagnose what's wrong with this team dynamic. Be specific, not abstract.
What's the single most important risk in this plan? Pick one.
Take a position on whether this technology will succeed. Defend it with specifics.
Engage with the strongest objection to this argument and rebut it.
Decide between two competing strategies and commit to one with a one-line justification.
Make a deployment call: ship now or wait two weeks. Pick one and defend it.
